stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a ethnic minority working class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with the wishes of her constituents, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a ethnic minority working class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with the wishes of her constituents, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
